title:
Lesson 1:
Don't Forget / I Forgot
description: 'Que no se te olvide' = Don't forget (literally: may it not slip your mind). 'Lo olvidé' = I forgot. 'Lo olvidé por completo' = I completely forgot. Very useful in daily life.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Que no se te olvide traer el documento mañana.
dialogue.english: Don't forget to bring the document tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Of course, I'll write it down right now.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you call the doctor today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Ay, lo olvidé por completo. Lo llamo ahora.
dialogue.english: Oh, I completely forgot. I'll call now.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Y la reunión del lunes, ¿la anotaste?
dialogue.english: And the Monday meeting, did you write it down?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Ese también lo olvidé. Necesito un calendario.
dialogue.english: I forgot that one too. I need a calendar.
